reference: What can I do for you? I I do have a COPD diagnosis which I was diagnosed with like a couple years ago, but this is this seems to be much worse. OK, you said you were diagnosed with COPD couple years ago. Was that two years ago? Three years ago? It was almost, probably five years ago. Five years ago, OK, and have you always had this cough since then? Yeah I’ve probably had a cough for the last 10 years, I think. OK. Yeah. And you you’ve mentioned that this cough has gotten worse over the last week? Yeah, I've been coughing a lot more. Even just walking down the stairs from my bedroom to even the kitchen I just have to take a little bit of a breather, just start coughing. OK. And is it a dry cough or are you bringing up any phlem? No, I'm bringing up like this. It's usually before this week. It was like kind of a clear to yellow. Sorry clear to white material, but this past week it's been more yellow in color. OK, any blood in there? No, I mean notice any blood. OK and you said you were also short of breath, uhm when did that start? So that's just in the last few days. OK. Alright, and do you get short of breath when you're laying down? Uhm no, not really, it's mainly just when I'm moving around and doing stuff. OK, how about when you're asleep? Does it wake you up and kind of make you gasp for air? No. No OK. So no orthopnea or PND. Alright, and the puffers that you're on, can you remind me which ones you're on again? So I'm on this one called Ventilin for the which I’ve been using a lot more recently just for relief and then, I'm on this other puffer thing. It's called spiriva. OK, how often? How? How many times this week have you had to use your ventilin? Uhm, Ventilin are probably used. Probably almost like 10 times this past week. OK. Alright, any chest pain? Uhm? Alright, and do you ever feel like your calves hurt after you walk awhile? No, not really. No OK, any dizziness or fainting spells? Uhm, No, no, not that I know. OK. Numbness or tingling? No. Confusion or memory loss? No. Headaches? No. Changes in your bowel movements? No. Uhm nausea or vomiting? Uh, no. Changes in your appetite? No, not really. Eating about the same. OK, changes in your urine? No. Runny nose or stuffy nose? Uh, no. I don’t have that. OK, sore throat? No. Any changes in your vision or hearing? No. Changes in your smell or taste? No, not really. OK, how about weight loss? No, my weights been pretty steady. OK, chills? Uhm, maybe a few times this, like from that last couple days I've been feeling a bit chilly. My father had lung cancer but he smoked a lot, I also smoke so I sometimes worry about that, but he had cancer and my mother, she was perfectly healthy. Yeah, so just my father. Just your father OK. How much do you smoke? Probably half a pack a day. It used to be one pack, even 2 packs at one point in time, but right now it's half the packaging. OK, alright, well, it's good that you're cutting down. And if you need more resources to help you do that, we have some available that we can refer you to. Couple times a month OK. And are you currently married? Yeah, I currently live with my sorry, wife, in an apartment. OK, OK, good, and have you been in contact with any sick people recently? No, not not really. Wie’ve been just to ourselves. OK, and have you traveled anywhere recently? No, just been on our house. OK. Alright, well those were all the questions that I had. Did you have any questions of your own? Uh, yeah I was just wondering what this can be. Yeah, you know it sounds like you have a cough and shortness of breath, and both of these things you've had for a while, but now they're getting worse and based on your past history of COPD I would say this sounds like a COPD exacerbation. And basically we should treat it we're going to take your oxygen saturation an give you some extra oxygen if you need. We’ll continue to use your bronchodilators an and in addition to both of that will also give you some steroids as well as antibiotics. And just to prevent any infections or if you already have it then it can help with that as well. And because your symptoms somewhat overlap with those of Covid will also do a covid swab just to make sure it's not that. And you should hear back about those results in 24 to 48 hours.
